user_query:
I'm preparing a study aid to help my immunology students connect our lesson topics with authoritative explanations. Please build a small workflow (script or notebook is fine) that orchestrates three things using the provided files and a web search step:

1) Read local files and extract concepts:
- Read input/lesson_outline.md and input/student_questions.csv.
- Identify up to 8 key immunology concepts to clarify by taking the case-insensitive intersection of phrases that appear in both files. Treat multi-word phrases from the outline bullet lines (e.g., innate immunity, adaptive immunity, antigen presentation, MHC, dendritic cells, B cells, T cells, clonal selection, cytokines, complement system, vaccination, immunological memory, hypersensitivity, tolerance, allergy, neutralizing antibodies, T helper cells) as candidate concepts and include only those that also appear in at least one student question. Deduplicate and rank by frequency in the questions; keep the top 8.

2) Modify the configuration before searching:
- Open config/search_config.yaml and edit it so that:
  - min_sources_per_concept is 2 (was 1).
  - allowed_domains includes at least 7 total distinct vetted domains relevant to immunology and biomedical education/research. Keep the existing entries and add at least five more from peer‑reviewed journals or official agencies (for example: cdc.gov, nejm.org, nature.com, cell.com, pnas.org, sciencemag.org, frontiersin.org, ecdc.europa.eu, ema.europa.eu). Do not remove nih.gov or who.int.
  - Keep output_dir as outputs (or set it to outputs if missing).
- Save the modified file and use it to constrain your searches.

3) Perform web searches and produce structured outputs:
- For each selected concept, perform search engine queries from the Internet restricted to the domains in allowed_domains from the finalized config. For each concept, choose at least two sources from distinct allowed domains in that list.
- Write the following outputs under the outputs/ directory:
  a) outputs/concept_index.csv with columns: concept, total_mentions_in_questions (integer), in_outline (true/false), in_questions (true/false). Include exactly the concepts you selected.
  b) outputs/concept_summaries.json with one JSON object per concept containing: 
     - concept (string)
     - sources (list of objects with source_domain and retrieved_title; source_domain must be one of the allowed_domains in the modified config)
     - summary (2–3 sentence, student‑friendly explanation you write in your own words)
     - analogy (one-sentence everyday analogy to aid understanding)
     - keywords (2–4 short terms drawn from the local files that help anchor the concept)
  c) outputs/search_log.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with: concept, query (the actual search string), chosen_urls (list), and timestamp (ISO 8601). Use this to show that each concept was searched and how sources were chosen.
  d) outputs/config_snapshot.yaml: a copy of the finalized config/search_config.yaml you used so the domain constraints can be verified locally.

Notes/constraints:
- The Internet step is required: gather sources by issuing search engine queries; do not paste long quotes—write your own summaries.
- All outputs must be reproducible from the local inputs plus your web queries and the modified config. Ensure each concept in outputs/concept_summaries.json has at least two sources from distinct domains that are present in outputs/config_snapshot.yaml.
- Do not hardcode direct URLs in this request; you may use any search engine of your choice during execution.

Deliverables summary: outputs/concept_index.csv, outputs/concept_summaries.json, outputs/search_log.jsonl, outputs/config_snapshot.yaml. Please keep all paths exactly as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
